title: SRS/Airbag Fault Reset After Repair
description: Clears SRS airbag diagnostic trouble codes after legitimate sensor or component replacement. CRITICAL SAFETY: Crash/deployment records are permanent and cannot be cleared — always flag module replacement requirement when deployment data is present.
common_symptoms: SRS warning light after accident sensor replacement
airbag light after clock spring replacement
seat belt pretensioner code after seat belt replacement
SRS light after steering wheel replacement
physical_solution: After replacing defective SRS component (seat belt pretensioner, crash sensor, clock spring, airbag module), connect scan tool to SRS module. Verify NO crash/deployment records are stored. If deployment record present — STOP, module must be replaced, not reset. If no deployment data, clear component fault code via scan tool. CRITICAL SAFETY RULES: (1) NEVER attempt to clear crash/deployment records (B0001-B0099 range typically). (2) If scan shows deployed airbag history — module replacement mandatory. (3) Crash data is a permanent safety record required by law in many jurisdictions. (4) SRS system must be live-tested after any repair before returning to customer.

technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Partial — SRS module address accessible on pre-gateway vehicles. Read operations ($19) accessible via ELM on most pre-2018 models. Clear ($14) may require security access ($27) on some OEMs — ELM limited for this step. NEVER attempt airbag reset without first confirming no deployment records exist.
preconditions: replacement component properly installed and torqued
no deployment/crash records stored in module
ignition ON, 10+ seconds wait for SRS capacitor discharge

procedure_steps.description: Address SRS module — Toyota 0x750, Honda SRS 0x18 (sub-address varies), BMW ACSM 0x1C, VW Airbag ECU 0x15, Ford RCM 0x738, GM SDM 0x54, Nissan Air Bag Sensor Unit 0x790, Stellantis ORC 0x744, Hyundai SRSCM 0x7A0
$10 03 — enter ExtendedDiagnosticSession at SRS module address
$19 02 FF — read all DTCs with any status mask. CRITICAL: examine full DTC list for deployment records before proceeding to any clear command

CHECK FOR DEPLOYMENT RECORDS — any codes in B0001-B0050 range with confirmed/testFailed status may indicate airbag deployment. If found: STOP, advise module replacement. Do NOT attempt to clear deployment data under any circumstances.

$19 04 [DTC high byte] [DTC mid byte] [DTC low byte] 01 — read freeze-frame snapshot for any suspicious DTCs to check crash event data (impact force, pyrotechnic squib resistance delta, deployment loop voltage)
$14 FF FF FF — clear DTCs only after confirming no deployment data present. Some OEMs require $27 security access seed/key exchange before this command is permitted.
$19 02 FF — re-read DTCs to confirm successful clear. Any remaining codes require further diagnosis of the physical SRS circuit.

Cycle ignition OFF then ON. Verify SRS warning light performs self-test blink then extinguishes within 5 seconds. Road test — verify SRS light remains OFF. Confirm all belt pretensioners reconnected. Document repair in vehicle history.

oem_notes.Toyota/Lexus: SRS ECU at address 0x750. Clear via $10 03 then $14 FF FF FF. Toyota SRS modules store impact data separately — check DTC B1610-B1615 range for deployment history. Lexus requires Techstream for ASSTR (airbag system) check on 2018+ models with advanced SRS architecture.
oem_notes.Honda/Acura: SRS module at 0x18 (sub-address varies by model). SRS-II system on 2013+. DTCs B0020-B0028 indicate squib circuits — verify not deployed before any clear attempt. Clock spring replacement very common — B1243/B1244 codes. ELM accessible on pre-2017 vehicles.
oem_notes.Nissan/Infiniti: Air bag diagnosis sensor unit at 0x790. Clear via extended session $10 03 then $14. B1000-B1003 codes indicate crash data stored — module replacement required, not reset. Most common fault: seat belt pretensioner code B1036 after minor collision.
oem_notes.Ford: Restraint Control Module (RCM) at 0x738. Ford SGW blocks external tool access on 2018+ — requires Ford FDRS or SGW bypass. B0001 is the deployment indicator — if set, RCM must be replaced. B0002 indicates seat belt pretensioner deployed. RCM cannot be cleared after deployment by any means.
oem_notes.GM: Sensing and Diagnostic Module (SDM) at 0x54. GM uses proprietary DTC format within B-code range. B0012-B0019 are frontal crash sensor codes. If AIR BAG DEPLOYED message appears in Driver Information Center (DIC), SDM replacement is mandatory. ELM CAN accessible on most GM platforms pre-2018.
oem_notes.BMW: ACSM (Advanced Crash Safety Module) at 0x1C on F-series platforms. ZGW gateway blocks extended diagnostic sessions without ISTA on F/G-series. BMW crash data is fully protected — if any crash event has been recorded in ACSM, module must be replaced per BMW repair instructions. Fault codes use AIRBAG01 through AIRBAGxx numbering.
oem_notes.VW/Audi: Airbag ECU (J234) at address 0x15. SFD (Security Feature Disable) protection on 2019+ blocks $14 clear command without ODIS authorization token. Crash data DTC 01217 (squib circuit triggered and recorded) indicates deployment — module replacement required. J533 CAN gateway present on all MQB platforms.
oem_notes.Stellantis: Occupant Restraint Controller (ORC) at 0x744. 2018+ SGW blocks all external write access — wiTECH 2.0 or authorized SGW bypass required. B0001-B0004 are deployment indicator codes. Pre-2018 ELM327 accessible for both read and clear. Crash data protection is federally mandated.
oem_notes.Hyundai/Kia: SRS Control Module (SRSCM) at 0x7A0. B1000-B1010 range indicates deployment data codes. Pre-2019 ELM327 fully accessible. 2019+ CAN gateway blocks write operations. GDS-Mobile recommended for complete SRS reset on 2019+ platforms. Genesis GV80 requires dealer-level security access for SRS module interaction.
